summary: First full test run of IT NOC Dashboard. All 26 backend tests pass. All frontend tabs load correctly with proper data. Dashboard shows 4 KPI cards, network map renders all site markers with animated dots, Settings page has kiosk page checkboxes. WUG topology returns 'pending' when unconfigured (expected).

ui_bugs.issue: HTML validity warning: `<span>` cannot be a child of `<option>` — React dev tools inject span wrappers inside select option elements. This causes a hydration-style console error but does NOT break functionality. Root cause: dynamic option rendering likely uses JSX expressions that get wrapped by dev tools.

issues: Map markers render as diamonds (◇) rather than circles as defined in MapEmbed.jsx — this may be a different map component used in /map page vs the dashboard mini-map which uses circles. Not a bug if intentional.

action_items: Minor: Review WazuhPage select/option rendering — avoid nesting non-text elements inside <option> tags to fix HTML validity console warning
critical_code_review_comments: server.py lifespan shutdown (line 759): wug_task is created but NOT included in the cancellation loop — potential task leak on shutdown

WUG alert auto-removal (line 1300-1302): modifying _alerts_store while iterating it via list() is safe, but alerts with source=wug that should NOT be removed (e.g., acknowledged ones) are still removed — no guard for acknowledged state

Settings YAML save_settings uses threading.Lock but load_settings acquires lock then calls _write_settings which does NOT acquire lock again (nested call path) — this is fine since _write_settings is only called from within locked context

success_rate.frontend: 95% (all tabs load, minor HTML validity issue on Wazuh page)

seed_data_creation: TEST_Alert and TEST_Delete_Alert created and deleted during backend tests
context_for_next_testing_agent: All core flows working. Backend 100% green. WUG/UniFi/Wazuh APIs expected to fail in preview (LAN-only) — this is correct. The wug_task created in lifespan is NOT cancelled on shutdown (minor leak). Wazuh page has HTML warning about span inside option. Map page uses a different component than MapEmbed.jsx with diamond markers and side panel — this appears intentional.
